beats.n_start: {CITY} on {RIVER}, the eve of summit day; you, drawn in {SELECTION_RITE}, are this year's {ROLE_TITLE}, the one young citizen who serves the river summit; {RIVER} runs low and brown under {BRIDGE}, barges sit aground at {HARBOR_FLATS}, and tomorrow the three courts that share the city, {ROUTE_A_COURT}, {ROUTE_B_COURT}, and {ROUTE_C_COURT}, meet at {SUMMIT_VENUE} over one question: {MYSTERY_Q}
beats.n_rules: {CHANCELLOR}, who will chair the summit, swears you in beneath the great bells and teaches you the envoy's three rules: carry messages truly, keep your ears open and your word good, serve the city and not one court; he shows you {ACCORD}, sixty years old, {ACCORD_PAGE} damaged and disputed, and lays out tomorrow: dawn, noon, and dusk sessions, each opened by a turn of {TIMEKEEPER}
beats.n_sort: {CHANCELLOR} lays three letters of attachment on the bench and names each: {ROUTE_A_COURT}, {ROUTE_A_DOMAIN}, its delegation led by {ROUTE_A_LEAD}, {ROUTE_A_LEAD_TRAIT}, whose {ROUTE_A_PROBLEM}; {ROUTE_B_COURT}, {ROUTE_B_DOMAIN}, led by {ROUTE_B_LEAD}, {ROUTE_B_LEAD_TRAIT}, whose {ROUTE_B_PROBLEM}; {ROUTE_C_COURT}, {ROUTE_C_DOMAIN}, under {ROUTE_C_LEAD}, {ROUTE_C_LEAD_TRAIT}, keeper of {ACCORD_PAGE}; each delegation needs its young envoy from tonight until the last bell, the courts will not share you, and {CHANCELLOR} says the choosing is yours alone
beats.t_hall: {ROUTE_A_HALL} smells of tar and ledger ink; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} sizes you up and hands you a runner's sash; out the window {HARBOR_FLATS} shows its mud like a bared secret, barges heeled over on their sides; if the summit fails, she says plainly, the harbor starves first, and she does not intend to let it
beats.t_walk: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} walks you down the quays naming every hull and what it carries; she tells you {CHANCELLOR} is sending his draft compromise to all three courts tonight by {COURIER}; near midnight, shouts rise from {BRIDGE}, where the harbor watch has stopped a limping courier under the lamps
beats.t_bridge: midnight on {BRIDGE}: {COURIER} leans on the split rail, ankle turned, and the seal on the chancellor's letter hangs torn; the watchman's lantern swings from face to face and somebody mutters the word {ROUTE_A_RUMOR}; three things could use doing and there is one of you
beats.t_a1: you stay at {COURIER}'s elbow and listen: he slipped on the wet steps, caught the rail, felt the seal tear on something sharp; his voice shakes but his story holds together; the watchman writes it down slowly while the crowd on the bridge grows, and the whisper of {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} grows with it
beats.t_a2: dawn session at {SUMMIT_VENUE}: {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER} and asks for grievances; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} rises and accuses {ROUTE_B_COURT} of taking the river with their new channels; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} answers quietly that his orchards are browning too; the shouting swallows the rest and {CHANCELLOR} suspends the session until noon
beats.t_a3: morning on the quays: the story of the torn seal has beaten you here, retold worse at every mooring; barge crews say {ROUTE_B_COURT} opened the chancellor's letter, or {ROUTE_C_COURT} wrote two versions of it; you know what you heard from {COURIER} himself, which makes every whisper itch
beats.t_a4: {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY}, {ROUTE_A_LEAD}'s deputy, pulls you behind a stack of crab pots: the captains are ready to believe {ROUTE_B_COURT} tampered with the letter, and one good push would put the whole harbor at {ROUTE_A_LEAD}'s back by noon; all {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} asks of the envoy who stood on that bridge is to let {TA_GATE} stand today
beats.t_a5: you tell {ROUTE_A_LEAD} {COURIER}'s account, the wet steps, the nail, the accidental tear; she listens without blinking and says the harbor argues for the river, not with lies; at noon the courts lay the accord copies side by side and {ACCORD_PAGE} is bared; then, mid-argument, {FLOOD_SIGNAL} begins to ring
beats.t_a6: {FLOOD_SITE} has jammed and water is backing toward {LOW_QUARTER}; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} is up in one motion, sending crews; she gives her envoy a choice of posts: the sluice gang needs runners at the water, and the tally room needs hands to ready {TA_TALLY} before the dusk session
beats.t_a7: {FLOOD_SITE}: brown water piling, {ROUTE_A_COURT} sailors and {ROUTE_B_COURT} wardens hauling the same rope; the jam comes up in armfuls of {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, no sabotage at all; and as the pool drains you glimpse, below the gate, {GAUGE} furred with silt
beats.t_a8: dusk session, one hourglass turn late; the hall smells of river mud off everyone's boots; {CHANCELLOR} calls for witnesses to the day, the bridge, the sluice, and the state of {ACCORD}, before any signing can start; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} catches your eye and nods you forward
beats.t_a9: you take the witness step with the envoy's sash straight; the room is a wall of faces, {ROUTE_A_LEAD}'s steady among them; you have three true things to tell: what {COURIER} said on the bridge at midnight, what the debris looked like coming out of the sluice, and {GAUGE} you saw under the gate
beats.t_ae1: you tell it plainly and the story of {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} dies in the room; {GAUGE} explains the drifted measures better than any accusation; {CHANCELLOR} steers the courts to terms, rebuild the gauge as a public one, share the dredging, water the groves at night; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} tells the bench the harbor is proud to have lent the city its envoy
beats.t_a10: the tally room: you and two clerks pull {TA_TALLY}; the numbers keep saying the same unwelcome thing, the river began dropping weeks before {ROUTE_B_COURT} ever opened their channels; whatever is wrong with {RIVER} started earlier and quieter than any feud
beats.t_a11: dusk session, one turn late; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} reads your log page to the hall, dates and drafts and nothing else; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} confirms the orchard wells match it; {CHANCELLOR} says evidence like this deserves measuring rather than arguing, and asks the courts what they mean to do with the truth
beats.t_ae2: no accord is signed tonight; instead the three courts order a joint survey of {RIVER} and {GAUGE}, its findings to be read publicly next month; it is not the ending anyone pictured at dawn, but it is honest ground; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} says slow water still reaches the sea, and has your log page entered into the record
beats.t_a12: you look away; by noon the quays believe {ROUTE_B_COURT} tore the seal, and the noon session curdles, captains jeering from the gallery while {ACCORD_PAGE} is shown; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} stops answering questions at all; then {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings and everyone is running
beats.t_a13: at {FLOOD_SITE} the rumor got there first: {ROUTE_A_COURT} haulers and {ROUTE_B_COURT} wardens grip the same rope like they would rather not share it, and the work goes ragged; your stomach knows whose push started this; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} is ten steps away, directing crews through the spray
beats.t_a14: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} goes very still, then says torn sails mend and torn trust takes longer, and marches you down the rope line to tell the captains {COURIER}'s true story yourself, twice, three times, until the gangs pull like one crew again; the jam comes up as plain {DEBRIS_CAUSE}
beats.t_a15: dusk, one turn late: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} stands and corrects the record herself, the harbor spread a false story and the harbor takes it back; {CHANCELLOR} notes the correction with visible respect; the talk turns at last to the real question, the drifted measures of a tired old {ACCORD}
beats.t_ae3: the courts sign a narrower accord tonight, rebuild {GAUGE} first and settle the shares at next season's summit, and {CHANCELLOR} credits the correction that made calm possible; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} tells you the hardest cargo she ever carried was an apology, and that you carried yours to the right dock in time
beats.t_a16: the rumor wins the rope line: a {ROUTE_B_COURT} warden is shoved, shovels drop, and grown folk have to be hauled apart by grayer grown folk while the water keeps rising; the jam clears late and ugly, and the dusk session opens with mud on everyone and trust on no one
beats.t_ae4: dusk collapses under too much anger and too little daylight; {CHANCELLOR} closes the summit without an accord, but before the hall empties he enters {COURIER}'s true account into the record, with you named as the bridge witness who confirmed it at last; next season starts from the truth, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} says on the dark walk home, thanks to that much, and she leaves the rest unsaid
beats.t_b1: you sprint the quay and hammer on {ROUTE_A_LEAD}'s door; she comes in sea boots, questions the watch herself, and has the letter's arrival written down then and there, witnessed by a {ROUTE_C_COURT} clerk and a {ROUTE_B_COURT} carter pulled off the street; whatever the seal looks like, she says, the record will be straight
beats.t_b2: dawn session: {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER}; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} accuses {ROUTE_B_COURT} of overdrawing the river, but when {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} first surfaces she reads the bridge record aloud and flattens it; the shouting ends the session anyway, yet you notice {CHANCELLOR} mark something in his notes with approval
beats.t_b3: back at {ROUTE_A_HALL}, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} lays two errands on the chart table and says an envoy should choose work the way a pilot chooses water, by where the hull actually sits; {ROUTE_C_COURT} have asked for {TB_TALLY_BOOK}, and {HARBOR_FLATS} needs its grounded barges measured before noon
beats.t_b4: {TB_TALLY_BOOK} is the harbor's sworn record of drafts and cargoes, wanted at {SUMMIT_VENUE} for checking against {ACCORD}; the barge measuring is cold mud work with {ROUTE_A_LEAD} herself, hunting exactly how far {RIVER} has fallen; both will matter by dusk, and there is one of you
beats.t_b5: {SUMMIT_VENUE} hums like a struck bell, clerks comparing accord copies at long tables; as you hand over {TB_TALLY_BOOK} you hear one mutter that {ACCORD_PAGE}s do not agree copy to copy; you carry that overheard sentence back across {BRIDGE} like a live coal in cupped hands
beats.t_b6: noon session: {ACCORD_PAGE} is laid bare and, just as the clerk muttered, the fair copies disagree; because your delivered {TB_TALLY_BOOK} sits open on the table, the courts can at least argue from the same harbor numbers; then {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rolls across the roofs and the hall empties at a run
beats.t_b7: {LOW_QUARTER}: water curling up through the drain grates, {FLOOD_SITE} jammed somewhere above; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} splits her crews without raising her voice, a sandbag line to hold the warehouse lane, and a runner chain to move true word between sluice, Hall, and harbor
beats.t_b8: the sandbag line: you pass weight until your arms are string, and {ROUTE_B_COURT} wardens shoulder in beside {ROUTE_A_COURT} sailors without anyone asking whose court owns which sandbag; behind you the lane stays dry; ahead, the shout goes up that the jam is {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, coming clear
beats.t_b8b: dusk, one turn late, mud to every knee in the hall; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} speaks last and shortest: the river does not care whose water it is, she says, and today the line held because every court held it; {CHANCELLOR} lets the words sit a moment before he opens the terms
beats.t_be1: the accord comes together like the sandbag line, one weight at a time: rebuild {GAUGE}, share the dredging, night water for the groves, retimed drafts for the barges; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} signs first and hands you the pen to carry to the other courts, which is envoy's work, done properly, in front of everyone
beats.t_b9: you run the chain, sluice to Hall to harbor and back, carrying counts and orders; on your third leg the sluice keeper hands you the report that matters, the jam is {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, no cut ropes and no sabotage, and you put it into {CHANCELLOR}'s own hand before dusk
beats.t_be2: the dusk session opens with your carried report read aloud, and the sabotage talk dies before it can poison the terms; the courts initial a working accord and set the full signing for {GAUGE}'s first public reading; {CHANCELLOR} notes for the record that a summit runs on true feet
beats.t_b10: {HARBOR_FLATS} at low water: you and {ROUTE_A_LEAD} measure heeled barges with rod and line while the mud sucks at your boots; {TB_FIGURES} keep saying the same unwelcome thing, the river began falling weeks before {ROUTE_B_COURT} ever opened their new channels; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} reads your column twice and goes quiet
beats.t_b11: with {ROUTE_A_LEAD} called ahead to the Hall, {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} catches you at the sea wall and holds out a hand for {TB_FIGURES}: those numbers soften the case against {ROUTE_B_COURT}, {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} says, and the harbor cannot afford soft today; better {TB_GATE} stays hidden until the summit is won
beats.t_b12: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} reads {TB_FIGURES}, jaw tight, and says the harbor does not trim its soundings; at noon she presents them herself alongside {ACCORD_PAGE}, which costs her case and buys her something better; when {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings she leads her crews out with her head up
beats.t_b12b: at {FLOOD_SITE}, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} works the debris line across from {ROUTE_A_LEAD}, and between hauls the two of them speak, actually speak, about wells and drafts and dry years; the jam comes up as plain {DEBRIS_CAUSE}; you watch two old rivals discover they have been losing the same river
beats.t_b12c: dusk, one turn late: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} present {TB_FIGURES} and the orchard well records together, two courts telling one story between them; {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER} and observes that for the first time all day the summit is measuring instead of blaming
beats.t_be3: the accord is signed on shared numbers: a rebuilt public {GAUGE} at {FLOOD_SITE}, shared dredging, night watering, retimed drafts; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} tells the hall that the envoy reminded the harbor of its own law, sound honestly and sail safely; outside, {RIVER} slides on toward the sea, unbothered and forgiven
beats.t_b13: noon session: a {ROUTE_C_COURT} surveyor presents {HARBOR_FLATS} soundings anyway, taken independently that morning, and asks why the harbor's own figures were not offered; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} is blindsided in front of the courts, {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} studies the floor, and {TB_GATE} burns like a stone; then {FLOOD_SIGNAL} interrupts everything
beats.t_b14: the flood work swallows the afternoon, sandbags and shouted counts, but the question is waiting when the water drops; before dusk, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} gathers her delegation on {SUMMIT_VENUE}'s steps to settle what the harbor will say about {TB_GATE}
beats.t_b15: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} takes the page without a word, walks in, and opens dusk by apologizing to the courts for her own house holding back soundings; it costs her, visibly; {CHANCELLOR} accepts the correction, the session steadies, and the argument finally turns to the measures themselves, though the daylight is nearly spent
beats.t_be4: too little hourglass remains for a full accord, so the courts order a joint survey and set a signing date, the harbor's late figures first into the new public ledger; on the walk home {ROUTE_A_LEAD} says the tide went out on the deal but the harbor got its name back, and that was the dearer cargo
beats.t_b16: dusk runs aground on distrust: if the harbor sat on its own soundings, the other courts ask, what else sits unoffered; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} fights for the terms stubbornly, but the room has hardened around her, and the hourglass runs out while the question is still circling
beats.t_be5: the summit closes without an accord; that night {ROUTE_A_LEAD} finds {TB_GATE} anyway, reads them twice by lamplight, and enters them in the harbor record under the next day's date; next year, she says, the truth goes first through the door; she does not scold you twice, but then she does not need to
beats.t_c1: you crouch by the rail with the watchman's lantern: {TC_THREAD} snagged where the rail split, the same weave as {COURIER}'s cloak; the tear in the seal matches the nail's bite exactly; you have the watchman log {TC_THREAD} and the hour, with your name as witness
beats.t_c2: dawn session: when {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} first slithers up the benches, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} reads the watch log aloud, {TC_THREAD}, hour, envoy's witness, and the whisper loses its legs; the session still breaks on the water question, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} accusing and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} denying, the hourglass turned and abandoned
beats.t_c3: mid-morning {COURIER} finds you on the quay, walking on a wrapped ankle, to say thank you; the letter went on to all three courts and {ROUTE_C_COURT} re-waxed the seal at their own door before witnesses; a small thing set straight, he says, but summits are stacked out of small things
beats.t_c4: two summonses arrive with one bell: {CHANCELLOR} asks the bridge witness to sit beside the chair for the day, a neutral post in every court's sight, and {ROUTE_A_LEAD} wants her sworn envoy at {ROUTE_A_COURT} bench where you were attached; {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} mutters that nobody can stand in two courts at once
beats.t_c5: noon from beside the chair: you see the summit the way {CHANCELLOR} sees it, three tired courts each certain it is the honest one; {ACCORD_PAGE} is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing; when {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings, {CHANCELLOR} keeps his seat one breath longer than everyone else, watching who runs where
beats.t_c6: {CHANCELLOR} needs two things at once during the scare and has one envoy: a steady voice at {SUMMIT_VENUE}'s door relaying the chair's orders both ways, or a pair of young eyes at {FLOOD_SITE} itself to bring back what is actually true down there
beats.t_c7: you hold the door and pass the chair's orders both ways, pumps to {LOW_QUARTER}, the tally of hands by court, the extra hourglass turn announced to the benches; through the doorway you watch three courts blur into one dripping crew, and you keep the ledger of who helped, which is everyone
beats.t_c8: dusk: {CHANCELLOR} opens by reading your door ledger aloud, every court's hands at the sluice, then asks you to state the bridge facts one last time for the record; a neutral witness, he tells the hall over his hourglass, is the rarest water in {CITY}
beats.t_ce1: on straight facts and a shared afternoon the courts settle terms by full dark: a public {GAUGE}, shared dredging, night water, honest soundings; {CHANCELLOR} signs last and tells you that every accord is only paper, and what holds is the witnessing; he says, turning the hourglass flat, that you witnessed well
beats.t_c9: you reach {FLOOD_SITE} as the jam gives way, {DEBRIS_CAUSE} and no cut ropes anywhere; below the draining gate you see it plainly and sketch it fast, {GAUGE} silted to the collar, the accord's own measure drowned in mud for years
beats.t_c10: {CHANCELLOR} studies your sketch of {GAUGE} a long time and says quietly that the city has been arguing over a ruler nobody could read; at dusk he sets the sketch before the courts and asks whether they would rather be right tonight or measured rightly forever
beats.t_ce3: the courts choose measured rightly: no full accord tonight, but a binding order to raise and rebuild {GAUGE}, signatures set for its first public reading; {CHANCELLOR} enters your sketch into the record and tells you that some days the bravest thing a summit can do is wait for the truth to dry out
beats.t_c11: you keep {ROUTE_A_COURT} bench, and {ROUTE_A_LEAD} puts you to proper envoy work between sessions, carrying the harbor's numbers to {ROUTE_C_COURT} and their queries back; at noon {ACCORD_PAGE} is laid bare; when {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} sends her crews and keeps her runner, you, at her side
beats.t_c12: the warehouse lane: sandbags, shouted counts, the harbor holding its own doorstep while word comes down the runner chain that the sluice jam is {DEBRIS_CAUSE} coming clear; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} works beside you and asks, between bags, exactly what you saw and logged on that bridge at midnight
beats.t_c13: dusk, one turn late: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} cites your bridge log to close {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} for good, then lays out the harbor's case; loyalty, she tells the bench with a hand on your shoulder, is not saying what your court wants, it is bringing your court the truth while there is still time to use it
beats.t_ce2: the courts initial a working accord, gauge first, full terms at next season's summit, and {ROUTE_A_LEAD} writes into the harbor log that this year's {ROLE_TITLE} served {ROUTE_A_COURT} best by staying exactly honest; you walk home along a river that has not changed beside a city that slightly has
beats.g_road: the orchard road out to {ROUTE_B_HALL} at dusk before the summit: terraces stepped up the valley, leaves curling early, {GB_CISTERN} showing their rings; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} meets you at the lodge with tea and no small talk: {ROUTE_B_COURT} dug new channels this spring because the rains failed twice, he says, and now the whole city says thief
beats.g_gate: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} walks you along the channels by lantern light: clay-lined, hand-dug, opened to save the young trees; he shows you the sluice-fed mains and the numbered gates with their pins; at {GA_GATE_NUM} the pin sits crooked in its hasp, as if lifted once and reset, and {ROUTE_B_LEAD}, ahead with the lantern, does not see it
beats.g_walk: from {ROUTE_B_HALL} you can see {BRIDGE} lamps across the fields; near midnight {COURIER} hurries over the bridge, lantern guttering, and goes down hard on the wet steps; he is up again, limping on toward {SUMMIT_VENUE}, before you can even call out; the chancellor's letters, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} murmurs, go out tonight
beats.g_night: the fallen {COURIER} limps on with his lantern failing and two fields still to cross; you could catch him with the lodge lantern and see him safely to {SUMMIT_VENUE}'s door, or wake {ROUTE_B_LEAD} so {ROUTE_B_COURT} can send proper word of the fall ahead to the Bell watch; the cold will not hold the choice open long
beats.g_a1: you catch {COURIER} at the field stile: ankle swelling, and the chancellor's seal torn where it caught a nail on the split bridge rail, a gray thread of his own cloak left behind on the metal; you lend him your shoulder as far as {SUMMIT_VENUE}'s door and hear the night clerk log him in
beats.g_a2: dawn session from {ROUTE_B_COURT} bench: {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER}; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} stands and accuses {ROUTE_B_COURT} of taking the river with their new channels, and half the gallery growls along with her; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} answers only that his orchards are browning too; the shouting drowns the rest and {CHANCELLOR} suspends until noon
beats.g_a3: in the market you hear the torn seal retold as theft, {ROUTE_B_COURT} opened the chancellor's letter, folk are saying, {ROUTE_B_COURT} tore the seal; you stood at the stile and know better; back at the channels, {ROUTE_B_CHAR} the under-warden is waiting for you alone, twisting her hat in her hands
beats.g_a4: {ROUTE_B_CHAR} confesses to you alone: in the worst dry week she lifted {GA_GATE_NUM} without leave, one night, to save the young trees, and reset the pin after; with the whole city already calling {ROUTE_B_COURT} thieves, she begs you to keep {GA_GATE} just until the summit is over, one day of silence, envoy, one day
beats.g_a5: {ROUTE_B_CHAR} tells {ROUTE_B_LEAD} with you standing beside her; he is quiet long enough for the orchard to hear it, then thanks her for the truth and grieves the timing; {GA_GATE_NUM} is closed and the lift logged with its dates, and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} says {ROUTE_B_COURT} will not be the court that hides things, he will disclose it at noon himself
beats.g_a6: noon session: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} rises first and discloses {GA_GATE_NUM} before anyone can accuse, one gate, one night, logged and closed; the hall rustles, but there is respect in the sound; then {ACCORD_PAGE} is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing, and into the middle of the argument {FLOOD_SIGNAL} begins to ring
beats.g_a7: {FLOOD_SITE} is jammed and backing toward {LOW_QUARTER}, and {ROUTE_B_COURT}, whose channels feed off that sluice, are watched by every eye on the bank; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} needs runners three ways at once and trusts his envoy to pick the post that needs you most
beats.g_a8: at {FLOOD_SITE} you haul rope beside sailors who eyed {ROUTE_B_COURT} all day; the jam comes up as {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, no cut ropes anywhere; and as the pool drains a gray-bearded {ROUTE_A_COURT} hauler whistles low: below the gate, silted to the collar, sits {GAUGE}
beats.g_a9: dusk session, one turn late, mud on every bench; when {CHANCELLOR} calls for what the day taught the city, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} sends you, the envoy who stood in the water, to the witness step: the debris, the shared rope, and {GAUGE} that nobody has been able to read for years
beats.g_ae1: your witness turns the summit from blame to measuring: the courts agree to raise and rebuild {GAUGE} as a public one, share the dredging, and water the groves at night; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} signs for {ROUTE_B_COURT} and tells you an orchard grows on patience and true measure, and so, it turns out, does a city
beats.g_a10: you walk the line with the gate book: every gate checked, pinned, and signed, {GA_GATE_NUM}'s closure fresh on its page; twice you turn away angry strangers hunting sabotage with lanterns, politely, with the book open; when the water settles, {ROUTE_B_COURT} ledger shows every drop accounted, dated, and initialed
beats.g_a11: dusk: when a {ROUTE_A_COURT} captain rises to suggest {ROUTE_B_COURT} used the scare to draw water, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} does not argue; he passes your gate book down the bench and lets the courts read it page by page until the muttering dies; honesty at noon, he murmurs beside you, is why the book is believed at dusk
beats.g_ae2: the courts initial a working accord, rebuild {GAUGE} first and set the shares by its first honest readings, and {ROUTE_B_COURT} walk out with their name scrubbed clean by their own bookkeeping; at the lodge {ROUTE_B_LEAD} tells the wardens their envoy guarded the orchard with a pen, which is how it is done in a city of courts
beats.g_a12: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} writes fast on the lodge table: {ROUTE_B_COURT} offer night watering, metered gates, and a share of the dredging costs, all of it contingent on a rebuilt public {GAUGE}; you run the offer through streets loud with flood work, past the sandbag lines, and put it into the hands of the chancellor's clerk before the dusk bell
beats.g_a13: dusk, one turn late: {CHANCELLOR} opens by reading {ROUTE_B_COURT} offer aloud, the first plain proposal of the whole summit, and the room changes temperature; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} stands, allows that the harbor could retime its barge drafts, and suddenly the courts are trading terms instead of accusations
beats.g_ae3: built on {ROUTE_B_COURT}'s opening bid, the courts initial an accord by lamplight and set the signing for {GAUGE}'s first reading; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} tells you an envoy's legs matter as much as an envoy's ears, and that tonight {ROUTE_B_COURT} led the city by offering first; the orchard road home smells of rain finally coming
beats.g_a14: you give {ROUTE_B_CHAR} your silence, and at noon it spends itself: a {ROUTE_C_COURT} surveyor reports fresh scratches on {GA_GATE_NUM}'s pin and asks {ROUTE_B_COURT} to explain, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} turns to his own bench blindsided, {ROUTE_B_CHAR} goes pale as paper beside you, and before anyone can answer anything, {FLOOD_SIGNAL} begins to ring
beats.g_a15: the scare falls hardest on {ROUTE_B_COURT}: every eye at the sluice asks whether the court that lifted a gate in secret also jammed a sluice; you haul debris beside {ROUTE_B_CHAR}, who will not look up from the rope, while {ROUTE_B_LEAD} carries the suspicion of the whole bank on his level voice
beats.g_a16: in the lull before dusk, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} gathers the lodge and asks plainly: does anyone know why {GA_GATE_NUM}'s pin was scratched; {ROUTE_B_CHAR}'s breath goes thin beside you; {GA_GATE} sits on your tongue like a pebble, and the hourglass at {SUMMIT_VENUE} is already being carried to the bench
beats.g_a17: you tell it, and {ROUTE_B_CHAR}, released, tells the rest herself; {ROUTE_B_LEAD}'s disappointment is a weather that passes; at dusk he discloses {GA_GATE_NUM} late, with {ROUTE_B_CHAR} standing beside him and you named as the envoy who would not carry a hidden thing one field farther
beats.g_ae4: the courts, wary now, sign nothing tonight; they order a joint survey of gates and {GAUGE} with {ROUTE_B_COURT} records open to all, and set a new summit for the month's end; {ROUTE_B_CHAR} keeps her post on probation and grips your hand hard at the lodge door; a promise to hide, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} says, is a seed that only grows downward
beats.g_a18: silence holds until dusk and then breaks everything at once: the surveyor presses, {ROUTE_B_CHAR} confesses in front of all three courts, too late for it to look like anything but concealment, and {ROUTE_B_COURT} case, honest on every other page, drowns in the one page it held back
beats.g_ae5: the summit closes without an accord; {CHANCELLOR} records {ROUTE_B_CHAR}'s confession, and at {ROUTE_B_LEAD}'s own asking he also records the envoy's kindness to a hurt courier on the summit's eve, so the record holds the whole of the day; walking home, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} says the orchard will stand one more dry season, then plants his words slowly: the hiding cost more than the water ever did
beats.g_b1: you wake {ROUTE_B_LEAD}; he sends a rider to the Bell watch at once, so the courier's fall on the bridge is logged by two courts before any rumor can be born crooked; a small thing, he says, pulling on his boots, but in a summit year you log the weather itself; you sleep three hours and dream of numbered gates
beats.g_b2: dawn session: {ROUTE_A_LEAD} accuses {ROUTE_B_COURT} of taking the river with their new channels; when {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} tries the air, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} cites the night log, courier fallen, seal torn on the rail, reported within the hour, and the whisper thins; the water question still ends in shouting, and {CHANCELLOR} suspends until noon
beats.g_b3: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} has two jobs and one envoy before the noon bell: ride the channel line with him, measuring the real flows against what the accusers claim {ROUTE_B_COURT} take, or help {ROUTE_B_CHAR} ready {GB_CISTERN}, {ROUTE_B_COURT}' stored rain, for a goodwill release to the starving lower river
beats.g_b4: gate by gate you measure: even opened wide, the new channels take less water than the harbor swears is missing; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} chalks each figure on the gate boards where anyone who walks the line may check it, and says that something upstream of everyone's certainty is wrong with the measuring itself
beats.g_b5: noon session: {ACCORD_PAGE} is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} presents your flow figures, less taken than claimed missing, and for one blessed minute the courts frown at the same numbers instead of at each other; then {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rolls through the hall
beats.g_b6: at the jammed sluice {ROUTE_B_LEAD} splits his wardens: a debris crew into the water beside {ROUTE_A_COURT} sailors, and a gate crew to hold and watch the channel mouths so that no surge can be blamed on {ROUTE_B_COURT} afterward; he leaves the choice of posts to his envoy
beats.g_b7: between hauls you sound the backed pool with the measuring rod, and when the debris comes clear, {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, no sabotage, your rod raps something square below the gate: {GAUGE}, silted blind, the accord's own measure that no living clerk has read
beats.g_b8: dusk, one turn late: your flow figures, your soundings, and {GAUGE} line up like beads on one string; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} lets the numbers speak first and then asks the courts a warden's question, whether they mean to keep farming blame or to start measuring water
beats.g_be1: the accord is signed around {ROUTE_B_COURT} arithmetic: raise {GAUGE}, read it publicly, night water for the orchards, shared dredging for the channel; {ROUTE_A_LEAD} shakes {ROUTE_B_LEAD}'s hand like a woman testing a fence post, and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} tells you the city was saved by a measuring rod and the patience to use it twice
beats.g_b9: you hold the gates, pinned, watched, and logged, while the surge shoulders past and {LOW_QUARTER} stays dry; twice a lantern party comes hunting {ROUTE_B_COURT} sabotage, and twice the open gate book turns them polite; when the jam clears as plain {DEBRIS_CAUSE}, your log shows the channels never moved
beats.g_b9b: dusk: when suspicion reaches for {ROUTE_B_COURT} one last time, the gate log answers it page by page, every pin, every hour, every initial; {CHANCELLOR} remarks that of all the day's evidence the dullest book is the most convincing, and asks the courts to talk terms at last
beats.g_be2: the courts initial a truce and a working accord, gauge first and shares after, and {ROUTE_B_COURT} gate book is entered whole into the record as the summit's example of an open ledger; at the lodge, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} tells the wardens that their envoy kept the gates, and the gates kept {ROUTE_B_COURT}' good name
beats.g_b10: {GB_CISTERN}: {ROUTE_B_COURT}' stored rain, saved all year for the harvest festival; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} means to release a share down-city as plain goodwill, proof {ROUTE_B_COURT} do not hoard while the river runs thin; you help {ROUTE_B_CHAR} rig the release gear and count off hands for the work
beats.g_b10b: noon session: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} announces the coming release; some benches call it neighborly and some call it a bribe, and then {ACCORD_PAGE} steals the argument entirely; when {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings, {ROUTE_B_LEAD} realizes aloud that a goodwill release into a backing river must now be timed exactly right or not at all
beats.g_b11: {ROUTE_B_CHAR}, hauling on the release gear, says the paperwork can catch up later, open {GB_GATE} now while the sluice pool is dropping and the gesture still lands today; the proper way is slower, log the release, notify {ROUTE_C_COURT}, and open on a witnessed signal, and the daylight is spending itself while you decide
beats.g_b12: the logged release opens on {ROUTE_C_COURT}' signal just as the cleared sluice can take it, and the fresh cistern water rides the drop harmlessly down-city; the sluice keeper marks the levels hour by hour into three ledgers, and {LOW_QUARTER} watches {ROUTE_B_COURT} send their festival water past their own orchards
beats.g_b12b: dusk, one turn late: the release is read into the record with its times and its levels, and even {ROUTE_A_LEAD} grants that water given on paper and in fact at once is a hard argument to distrust; the courts warm by degrees, like a hall filling slowly with people
beats.g_be3: a narrower accord is initialed tonight, {GAUGE} rebuilt first and full shares at next season's summit, and the record names {ROUTE_B_COURT} release as the turn of the day; {ROUTE_B_LEAD} tells you that generosity, logged, is called diplomacy, and that the festival will taste better for the water it gave away
beats.g_b13: {GB_GATE} open unlogged, and for one giddy hour it looks like generosity; then the sluice readings, already tangled by the jam, jump where no reading should, and a {ROUTE_C_COURT} surveyor asks acidly whether {ROUTE_B_COURT} are moving unrecorded water in the middle of a flood scare, which is, you realize coldly, exactly what happened
beats.g_b13b: {ROUTE_B_LEAD} spends the dusk hour untangling honest generosity from what the ledgers make it look like, and the accounting eats the summit's last daylight; {ROUTE_B_CHAR} owns her shortcut out loud before the courts, and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} will not let her carry it alone: the warden signs for every gate, he says, even the kind ones
beats.g_be4: the summit adjourns without an accord; by lamplight {ROUTE_C_COURT} re-tally the levels and enter the release, hours late, as the gift it truly was, and {CHANCELLOR} sets a new summit for the month's end, writing one line the whole city repeats by morning: a good deed done in the dark still needs a ledger
beats.b_hall: {SUMMIT_VENUE} at evening: lamplit tables, ladders in the stacks, and {ROUTE_C_LEAD}, who shakes your hand like a man filing it for reference; he shows you the trouble itself, {ACCORD}, sixty years old, {ACCORD_PAGE} water-stained and torn at the fold, and its two fair copies disagreeing with each other
beats.b_door: midnight door duty: {COURIER} arrives limping, the chancellor's letter in hand and its seal torn; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} logs the hour, the nail-shaped tear, and {COURIER}'s account of the wet steps and the split bridge rail, then re-waxes the packet before three witnesses so no one can ever say {ROUTE_C_COURT} read it; you write the label
beats.b_vault: tomorrow, {ROUTE_C_LEAD} says, the courts will fight over what {ACCORD} says, so tonight {ROUTE_C_COURT} must find out what it said; the answer sleeps somewhere in the stacks, in the accord-year copy drafts or in the sluice keepers' logbooks, and there is one of you and one dawn coming
beats.b_stacks: two trails lead into the dark of the stacks: the copy-drafts crate from the accord year, every scribe's working sheet the fair copies were made from, or the sluice keepers' logbooks, the daily readings of {FLOOD_SITE} gauge going back a century; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} will work whichever trail you do not choose
beats.b_c1: dawn session happens under your feet, muffled shouting and the hourglass bell, while you and {ROUTE_C_LEAD} unlace the accord-year crate: sixty years of dust, working drafts in two different hands, margin notes by scribes long dead; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} reads the shouting below by its rhythm alone: grievances, he says, suspended until noon
beats.b_c2: mid-morning you find it: the water-measure clauses drafted in one hand and fair-copied in another, and where the drafts read one way, the fair copy skips {BC_LINE}; in the margin of the flawed page, small and young, a copyist's initials; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} goes very quiet, and then says the name: {ROUTE_C_CHAR}
beats.b_c3: {ROUTE_C_CHAR}: senior clerk of {ROUTE_C_COURT}, sixty years at the long tables, due to retire with honors at the year's end; {BC_LINE} is his, made when he was your age, and it is why the two fair copies disagree and why each court can read the page its own way; the flaw is small; the consequence is this summit
beats.b_c4: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} himself finds you among the drafts, reads his own young initials, and grips the crate edge with both hands; he asks for {BC_GATE}, let him retire first, he will set the record right quietly after the summit, the courts need never know whose hand slipped sixty years ago
beats.b_c5: {ROUTE_C_LEAD} hears it standing very straight, and then surprises you both: the record is not ours to spare ourselves, he says, but a slip is not a sin, and he sets {ROUTE_C_CHAR}, whose eyes know the accord-year hands better than anyone alive, to lead the reconstruction of the true page from his own old drafts
beats.b_c5b: noon session: the reconstruction, drafts pinned beside the flawed fair copy, is shown to the courts; read true, the page hangs everything on readings from {FLOOD_SITE} gauge, a gauge, somebody says slowly into the hush, that no living clerk remembers reading; and into that silence comes {FLOOD_SIGNAL}
beats.b_c6: the scare strikes {ROUTE_C_COURT} where they live: the archive cellar, where the records sleep below river level, and {ROUTE_C_LEAD} is needed in three places at once; he looks at his envoy and lets you choose the post
beats.b_c7: the cellar brigade: crates up the spiral stair hand to hand, and when {ROUTE_C_COURT} run short of hands, {ROUTE_A_COURT} sailors and {ROUTE_B_COURT} wardens fill the line without being asked; the accord-year crate rides up between a fisherman and an orchard girl, and {ROUTE_C_LEAD} remarks that this is the first shared work the summit has done
beats.b_c8: dusk, one turn late: the reconstruction is presented, and {ROUTE_C_CHAR} himself rises to read the correction aloud, his own sixty-year-old slip named in his own steady voice; the hall does not laugh; several very senior heads bow slightly, the way you salute a hard thing done properly
beats.b_ce1: with the page read true the terms nearly write themselves: rebuild the public {GAUGE} the accord always meant, share the dredging, night water, open ledgers; {ROUTE_C_CHAR} signs as witness with a steady hand, and {ROUTE_C_LEAD} tells you the archive did not save the summit, it only told the truth on time, which is the same thing
beats.b_c9: the sluice: debris hauled clear by mixed crews, and there below the draining gate {ROUTE_C_LEAD} points with his cane, silt to the collar, {GAUGE} the true page depends on, drowned and unread for years; you sketch it fast while he measures the silt line, both of you soaked through and neither of you caring
beats.b_c10: dusk: the reconstruction and your sketch tell one story between them, the accord was sound and its instrument was lost; the argument was never thief against thief, it was a city measuring with its eyes shut; {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER} and asks the courts for terms that open them again
beats.b_ce2: the courts initial the gauge accord: raise it, rebuild it, read it publicly at every festival, the shares set by its first honest year; the signing is fixed for the first reading, and {ROUTE_C_LEAD} enters your sketch beside {ROUTE_C_CHAR}'s correction, because the flaw and the fix, he says, belong on the same shelf
beats.b_c11: you stay drafting with the chancellor's clerk, turning bench shout and legal growl into terms a barge captain and an orchard warden could both read aloud; the clerk works your plain sentences in between the formal ones and mutters that half of law is finding the words the anger can agree to
beats.b_c12: dusk: {CHANCELLOR} reads the draft terms, plain words first and formal after, and the plain words do the work, heads nodding along the benches before the jurists can even object; you watch three tired courts hear one sentence mean one thing, the same thing, for the first time all day
beats.b_ce3: the accord is signed in both registers, the formal copy for the shelves and the plain copy for the doors, and copies are posted at {ROUTE_A_HALL}, {ROUTE_B_HALL}, and {SUMMIT_VENUE} where anyone may read what was promised; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} tells you the archive keeps a special fondness for documents people actually understand
beats.b_c13: you give {ROUTE_C_CHAR} {BC_GATE}, and noon spends it cruelly: the flawed page is fought over line by line while the true drafts sit dark in their crate; suspicion, given nothing to eat, eats the courts instead; then {FLOOD_SIGNAL}, and in the cellar scramble you find {ROUTE_C_CHAR} alone, wrestling the accord-year crate toward a hiding place, water already at his shins
beats.b_c14: his eyes meet yours over the crate: help me set it aside, he says, just until my papers are through; the water climbs another stair; above you the brigade is calling for every pair of hands it can get
beats.b_c15: the brigade answers your shout; the crate goes up wet but whole, and the whole story comes up with it, drafts, {BC_LINE}, young initials; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} is cold with you for one flight of stairs and fair for all the rest; {ROUTE_C_CHAR}, wrung out, agrees the reconstruction happens tonight, late, or never
beats.b_c16: dusk gets the correction dripping and an hour past its moment; {CHANCELLOR} grants one extra turn of {TIMEKEEPER} so the reconstruction can be read in full; the courts, twice interrupted and once misled, listen with folded arms, believing the page now and trusting the day rather less
beats.b_ce4: the true page enters the record but the signatures do not: the courts adjourn to verify the drafts and will resume in a month; {ROUTE_C_CHAR}'s slip is recorded beside his sixty years of service, which is how {ROUTE_C_LEAD} insists it be written, and you are named as the envoy who shouted for help, which, he says, is the whole job, only sooner next time
beats.b_c17: the crate goes into the dark behind the boundary-stone shelf, and dusk goes down with the flawed page still ruling: the courts read it three ways, trust no reading, and the summit collapses politely, like a tent; within a week, you know, someone will find a water-marked crate exactly where honesty did not put it
beats.b_ce5: the summit fails, and days later the drafts surface anyway, as hidden things do, and the slip looks like a scheme until {ROUTE_C_LEAD}, who has guessed the shape of it, sets the record straight and retires {ROUTE_C_CHAR} with his honor bruised but whole; the archive forgives slips, {ROUTE_C_LEAD} tells you at the door, it is silences it cannot file
beats.b_k1: the keepers' logbooks: a century of {FLOOD_SITE} gauge readings in patient columns, and then, nineteen years back, the columns simply stop, mid-page, mid-month; dawn session mutters up through the floorboards, {ROUTE_A_LEAD} accusing and {ROUTE_B_LEAD} denying, while you hunt for the reason a river's diary just ends
beats.b_k2: the last keeper's final note, in a tired hand: gauge silted past reading, dredge requested again, no funds again; will measure from {BK_PIER_MARK} until the gauge is raised; and after that, nothing; nineteen years, you realize, of the city's water law hanging from an unofficial scratch on a bridge
beats.b_k2b: {ROUTE_C_LEAD} reads the note twice between sessions and puts on his coat: if the law has been living on a bridge pier, he says, then {ROUTE_C_COURT} had better go and pay their respects; the morning is bright and hard as the two of you cross toward {BRIDGE}
beats.b_k3: on the bridge {ROUTE_C_LEAD} finds {BK_PIER_MARK}, and you find something else where the rail split: {BK_THREAD}, the same weave as {COURIER}'s cloak; the seal tore here, on this nail, by accident, and you can prove it
beats.b_k4: two finds in one morning: {BK_PIER_MARK} that has been carrying the law, and {BK_THREAD} that clears {COURIER}; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} is already sketching the mark for the record, but {BK_GATE} is yours, and how you spend it is an envoy's own choice
beats.b_k5: the watch matches {BK_GATE} to cloak and nail to tear before the noon bell, and posts the finding; {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} dies at midday instead of festering; the noon session, spared one poison, argues the real one, {ACCORD_PAGE}, until {FLOOD_SIGNAL} rings and word comes that the backing pool is climbing the bridge piers
beats.b_k6: {BK_PIER_MARK}, the only measure the city has used for nineteen years, is going underwater while the courts still need it read against today's level; at the sluice the crews are launching a flat-bottomed boat onto the backed pool
beats.b_k7: you go out in the boat with a lantern and a keeper's rule, reading the mossy line against the climbing water while debris bumps the hull and the sluice crews haul {DEBRIS_CAUSE} out behind you; you call the figures and a {ROUTE_C_COURT} clerk on the bank writes them down, double-checked, signed, and splashed
beats.b_k8: dusk, one turn late: the keeper logs, {BK_PIER_MARK} reading, and {GAUGE} line up into one plain story, the city has been measuring its river law off a scratch, in good faith, for nineteen years; the hall goes very quiet, and {CHANCELLOR} turns {TIMEKEEPER} gently, as if it might wake something
beats.b_ke1: the accord is signed around your soaked figures: raise {GAUGE}, retire {BK_PIER_MARK} with honors, read the river publicly each season; {CHANCELLOR} has the last keeper's final note copied into the accord itself, so that no request for dredging is ever shelved unheard again; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} calls it the finest day of filing in his life
beats.b_k9: you wait for still water, and when the jam clears and the pool settles at lamplighting you read {BK_PIER_MARK} with two sworn witnesses and a level line, slower than heroics and twice as signed; the figures walk into the dusk session with three names on them and no mud on any
beats.b_ke2: dusk runs long by lamplight, but numbers carrying three signatures are hard to shout at: the courts initial the gauge accord and fix the full signing at {GAUGE}'s first reading; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} enters your measurement sheet as the summit's steadiest page, done, he notes, at the speed of being right
beats.b_k10: {BK_GATE} stays in your pocket, and {ROUTE_A_RUMOR}, unanswered, spends the day growing teeth: by noon the tamper story is fact in three flavors, and during the flood scare the watch, pressed to be seen doing something, searches {COURIER}'s satchel on the open street while the crowd watches him hold still and take it
beats.b_k11: {COURIER}'s face in the lantern light stays with you through the flood work; dusk and your grand moment are one hourglass away, and {BK_GATE} weighs a very great deal for a thread
beats.b_k12: you produce {BK_GATE} and say plainly when you found it; the watch sergeant looks at you for a long moment, then posts the finding and clears {COURIER} before the dusk bell; {ROUTE_C_LEAD}, informed, says only that evidence is for the record and not for the stage, and puts you to work hauling keeper logs to the hall
beats.b_ke3: dusk mends what it can: {COURIER} publicly cleared, the keeper logs and {BK_PIER_MARK} entered into the record, but the courts, rubbed raw by a day of rumor, adjourn to resume in a month rather than sign tonight; {COURIER} finds you after and shakes your hand anyway, which is somehow heavier than the hourglass
beats.b_k13: you stand at dusk and produce {BK_GATE} with a flourish, and it lands exactly wrong: a day too late for {COURIER} and an hour too clever for the courts; if the envoy sat on this, a captain asks aloud, what are the courts sitting on; the question has no bottom, and the session slides into it
beats.b_ke4: the summit closes unsigned; {CHANCELLOR} enters the nail, {BK_GATE}, and the hour you found them into the record, the truth preserved and the timing noted; {ROUTE_C_LEAD} walks you home under the cold bells and assigns you a season of door duty, kindly, because at the door, he says, everything that matters arrives on time or not at all

n_start.c_n_start_1: Climb {SUMMIT_VENUE}'s steps to be sworn in.

t_bridge.c_t_bridge_1: Stay close and listen to {COURIER}'s account.

t_a1.c_t_a1_1: See {COURIER} on his way and snatch some sleep.

t_a3.c_t_a3_1: Head in when {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} beckons you aside.

t_a4.c_t_a4_1: Refuse, and tell {ROUTE_A_LEAD} what you actually heard.
t_a4.c_t_a4_2: Look away and let {TA_GATE} do its work.

t_a13.c_t_a13_1: Tell {ROUTE_A_LEAD} {ROUTE_A_RUMOR} is false and you let it run.

t_b11.c_t_b11_2: Let {ROUTE_A_DEPUTY} pocket the page for now.

t_b14.c_t_b14_1: Own it: hand {ROUTE_A_LEAD} {TB_GATE} and the truth.

g_a1.c_g_a1_1: Take the dawn bell at {ROUTE_B_COURT} bench.

g_a4.c_g_a4_1: Say {ROUTE_B_LEAD} must hear it, and go with her.

g_b10b.c_g_b10b_1: Hurry back to the cistern gear with {ROUTE_B_CHAR}.

g_b11.c_g_b11_2: Skip the paperwork and open {GB_GATE} now.

b_c4.c_b_c4_1: Bring it to {ROUTE_C_LEAD} now, together with {ROUTE_C_CHAR}.

b_c6.c_b_c6_2: Go with {ROUTE_C_LEAD} to the sluice to see {GAUGE} itself.

b_k4.c_b_k4_1: Take {BK_GATE} to the watch now and kill the rumor.
